title: crassula exilis subsp. schmidtii
intro: Crassula exilis subsp. schmidtii, also known as the dwarf jade plant, is a rare and endangered succulent species native to South Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Crassula exilis subsp. schmidtii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's extracts in various medicinal formulations.
